name: SOL Session Drive Continuation v1
thesis: SOL 1h is the best-fit basket lane for testing whether an impulse hour that establishes structure can generate follow-through on the next bar when the move holds instead of fully mean reverting. The edge should come from session-drive continuation with broad structural hold, not fragile single-bar breakout grammar.
invalidation_condition: Abort if walk-forward testing shows zero trades on valid data, contradictory role behavior, weak blind-window participation, or no post-cost edge.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: An impulse hour establishes a directional drive and the next bar holds enough structure to allow continuation.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: In transition regimes, early impulse bars can create inventory imbalance that persists briefly if price does not fully mean revert on the next bar.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: SOL 1h is better than SOL 4h for this mechanism because session-drive behavior is inherently faster and should generate materially denser participation.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If the impulse bars mostly fully mean revert within one bar, the hold filter may still be too restrictive.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If participation exists but density is low, relax the hold-distance threshold before changing exits.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; structure, hold, and persistence roles align
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: reduced by moving this thesis to 1h instead of forcing it on 4h

long: Time-based de-risk after 3 bars if unrealized gain < 0.3 * ATR_14
short: Time-based de-risk after 3 bars if unrealized gain < 0.3 * ATR_14
